message:
fcp-core: harden RevocationRegistry::add_revocation against replay-to-defer attacks

Previously `add_revocation` unconditionally overwrote any existing
entry for an `object_id`. An attacker with owner-key access could
suppress an active revocation by emitting a second, well-formed
RevocationObject for the same object_id with `effective_at = u64::MAX`
(or any far-future time). The subsequent `is_revoked_at(now)` check
would then return false, because it compares `now >= effective_at`
and the new entry's effective_at is in the future — the original
revocation is silently gone from the registry.

Fix: keep the entry with the **earliest** `effective_at` on insert
conflict. An earlier revocation is strictly stricter (revoked for
more of the timeline), so it is always the safe side to prefer; a
later one can never re-activate the object.

Changes in crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs:

* `add_revocation`: replace the unconditional `insert` with a match
  on the existing entry. If one exists and its `effective_at` is
  `<= revocation.effective_at`, keep it; otherwise overwrite. The
  `<=` (not `<`) keeps the current entry on an exact tie, which
  avoids unnecessary churn for idempotent replays.
* Doc-comment on `add_revocation` updated to describe the invariant
  and the attack it defends against (replay-with-future-effective-at
  to suppress an active revocation).
* New test `registry_later_revocation_does_not_defer_active_one`:
  inserts an active (effective_at = 1_700_000_000) revocation, then
  a later one with effective_at = u64::MAX, and asserts the stored
  effective_at is still 1_700_000_000 and
  `is_revoked_at(now=1_700_000_001)` returns true.
* New test `registry_earlier_revocation_replaces_later_one`: the
  mirror case — insert the far-future entry first, then the stricter
  earlier one, and assert the earlier one wins. Guards the
  symmetrical path through the match.

No public API change; `get_revocation` / `is_revoked` / `is_revoked_at`
all keep their existing signatures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
